name: Malassezia Dermatitis
description: Malassezia pachydermatis is a lipophilic yeast that normally inhabits dog skin and ear canals. Overgrowth caused by allergies, hormonal disease, or skin fold moisture leads to intensely pruritic, greasy, malodorous skin dermatitis typically in skin folds and ears.
symptoms: Redness in skin folds and between toes
treatment_overview: Antifungal shampoos (miconazole, chlorhexidine) used 2-3 times weekly. Oral antifungals (itraconazole, ketoconazole) for severe cases. Treating underlying cause (allergies, hypothyroidism) prevents recurrence.
